# Go Dependency Injector

> A lightweight, type-safe dependency injection container and IoC framework for Go using generics.

Go Dependency Injector provides a service container that leverages Go generics for compile-time type safety, automatic constructor injection, and a clean API. It supports transient, singleton, and scoped lifetimes, detects circular dependencies with clear error messages, allows named registrations for multiple implementations of the same interface, and is fully thread-safe. Zero external dependencies.

## Links

- [Documentation](https://pegasusheavy.github.io/go-dependency-injector/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/pegasusheavy/go-dependency-injector)
- [pkg.go.dev](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/pegasusheavy/go-dependency-injector)

## Key Features

- Type-safe generics with `Register[T]()` and `Resolve[T]()` for compile-time type checking
- Multiple lifetimes: Transient (default), Singleton, and Scoped
- Automatic constructor parameter resolution from the container
- Circular dependency detection with clear error messages
- Named registrations for multiple implementations of the same interface
- Thread-safe for concurrent access across goroutines
- Zero external dependencies (Go standard library only)

## Installation

```bash
go get github.com/pegasusheavy/go-dependency-injector
```

Requires Go 1.22 or later.

## Quick Start

```go
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "github.com/pegasusheavy/go-dependency-injector/di"
)

type Logger interface {
    Log(message string)
}

type ConsoleLogger struct{}

func (l *ConsoleLogger) Log(message string) {
    fmt.Println("[LOG]", message)
}

func main() {
    container := di.New()

    di.Register[Logger](container, func() Logger {
        return &ConsoleLogger{}
    }, di.AsSingleton())

    logger := di.MustResolve[Logger](container)
    logger.Log("Hello from DI!")
}
```
